The basic structure of each risk model relies on the same demographic factors and HCC structures, but the relative weights 
they assign to each factor differ. CMS assigns beneficiaries a “resolved” payment risk score based on the beneficiary’s status in 
a payment month. In general, the status is a function of ESRD status, new enrollee status, and community or institutional 
status. CMS may also base risk score resolution on dual Medicare-Medicaid eligibility status. CMS adjusts model
